chemistry.scope_summary: Various aryl-alkyl and diaryl sulfides prepared from arylamines and thiols. Meta- and para-substituted substrates gave good to excellent yields. Ortho-substituted reactants showed slightly diminished yields. Alkylthiols exhibited similar reactivities as arylthiols. N-heterocyclic reactants resulted in somewhat lower yields. Compatible with aryl halides.

optimization_table.key_findings: tBuONO as nitrite source with [Ru(bpy)3]Cl2·6H2O catalyst under light gives optimal results

figures.mechanism_type: comparison of C-S bond formation methods
figures.caption: Scheme 1.  Comparison of various methods for constructing aromatic C – S bonds.
Scheme 2.  Substrate scope of the photocatalytic Stadler–Ziegler process.

figures.flow_path: Two syringes feed into capillary microreactor with LED irradiation, producing thioether product
figures.key_information: Blue LED light irradiation, safer handling of diazonium intermediates, homogeneous irradiation, short reaction times
figures.caption: Figure 1. Schematic representation of the microfluidic setup

figures.trend: Diazosulfide 5 peaks early (~1h) then decreases to zero, while Sulfide 3 increases after light exposure and plateaus at ~75%. Disulfide 4 remains relatively constant at ~20-25%.
figures.caption: Figure 2. HPLC yields of sulfide 3, disulfide 4, and diazosulfide 5 over time (0–5 h)

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Longer reaction time (60 min), lower yield (59%), safety concerns with explosive diazosulfide intermediate accumulation
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Shorter residence time (15 min), higher yield (79%), improved safety due to minimal intermediate accumulation, 78-fold improvement in throughput
translation_logic.what_changed: Reaction time reduced from 60 to 15 minutes, yield increased from 59% to 79%, scale increased from 1.0 to 13.2 mmol, light source changed from fluorescent to blue LED
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Temperature (25°C), concentration (0.1 M), solvent (MeCN), atmosphere (argon), catalyst and additive system
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Minimized accumulation of explosive diazosulfide intermediate through continuous processing

figure_descriptions: Reactor scheme showing two-inlet syringe pump setup with capillary microreactor and LED irradiation
Kinetic plot showing product formation over time for different species
Mechanistic schemes comparing C-S bond formation methods
Substrate scope table with 14 examples and yield range 21-91%

conflicts: Pump inlets: text says 1 inlet but figure clearly shows 2 syringes feeding the reactor - using figure value of 2
notes: High quality data extraction with clear batch vs flow comparison. Minor conflict resolved using figure data over text for pump inlet count. Time reduction factor calculated as 60/15 = 4.0. Yield improvement calculated as (79-59)/59 * 100 = 33.9%.
